methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $205M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B biotech company that has raised $205M to date. The valuation analysis draws on two applicable methods within a comparative universe of 4 private peers in biotech and health at same or adjacent stages, supplemented by 3 public sector comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $390M. Peer-median analysis across same-sector comparables—Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M, Series B)—produces a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range spanning $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied given absence of revenue disclosure, a standard constraint for private biotech.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $331M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide spread reflects material heterogeneity in peer stage and presumably technology maturity within the four-company sample. Key uncertainties include pipeline development status relative to peers, clinical advancement timelines, and whether comparables truly reflect Strand's capital efficiency or competitive positioning. Stage-based methodology anchors the lower bound; peer multiples dominate the upper bound.
